definition: Treasure Maps are craft-specific, living, self-updating guides that replace the traditional business plan. A Treasure Map is short (fits on one page), specific (exact materials, prices, links for each craft), and progressive (5 steps: Materials → First Piece → Set Up Project → Get First Backers → Scale). Unlike a business plan written for investors, a Treasure Map is written for the member doing the work. It updates in real-time: when Step 1 completes, the Map highlights Step 2; when the first backer arrives, real earnings data replaces projections. Progress persists across sessions. The Adaptive Experience-Gated Onboarding Router (Innovation #1951) routes members to the correct starting step based on where they are in their journey (Just an idea / Making things / Already selling). Each Map contains a craft-specific Economics Panel showing income trajectories at 50 / 500 / 5,000 backers. Treasure Maps are Cold Start tools: a member who completes through Step 4 has materials, a product, a project page, a Cue Card, and five backers — a functioning micro-business in weeks. At scale, 100 members following Treasure Maps simultaneously creates 100 micro-businesses generating platform economic activity.
ratification_chronology: Pudding #54 drafted Bishop B061 (April 2, 2026). Knight Session 81 (Treasure Map Progression), Knight Session 118 (Treasure Map Flow), Knight Session 164 (Treasure Map Flip Cards), Knight Session 311 (V2 Treasure Map Builder) all represent progressive build-out. Trail-head seeding migration 20260502000001 (BP015).
founder_voice_quotes: "Help each other help ourselves" — Golden Key of the cooperative; Treasure Maps operationalize this for the creator journey

definition: Code Breakers is the Liana Banyan engagement mechanic that rewards attention rather than extracting it. Across all documents (letters, articles, academic papers, cue cards), hidden keywords called Golden Keys are embedded. Three difficulty tiers: Fledgling (🪶 — word appears naturally, 1 feather), Flight (🪶🪶 — first letters of sentences spell the key, 3 feathers), Murder (🪶🪶🪶 — section headings spell the key, 5 feathers). Finding a Golden Key earns Crow Feathers; feathers convert to MARKS (governance currency). Non-zero-sum: when one person finds a key, it does NOT disappear — every finder earns rewards. Keys INCREASE in value as more people find them (Glowing tier mechanics — see Glowing Golden Keys synthesis). Re-stamping system: revisit after 7/30/90 days for 25%/10%/5% repeat feathers. Five document circles: Press Articles, Influencer Letters, Academic Papers, Critique Responses, Cue Cards. Milestone bonuses: 100 MARKS (first key), 500 MARKS + Badge (10 keys), 1,000 MARKS + Badge (Full Circle), 2,000 MARKS + Badge (Full Murder), Medallion + 5,000 MARKS (Grand Slam — all 45 keys). Comprehension questions also available (3-7 feathers; worth MORE than pattern keys because understanding matters more than scanning).
composing_primitives: Golden Key keyword system (hidden in plain text)
Crow Feathers (intermediate unit before MARKS conversion)
MARKS governance currency (earned through effort, not purchased)
Non-zero-sum reward design (no scarcity of keys)
Midas Touch tokens (Glowing key → Credit-to-Joule conversion ability)
ratification_chronology: Article 'Why Code-Breakers Matter' (ARTICLE_WHY_CODE_BREAKERS_MATTER_HUMANIZED.md) ratified and published. Pudding #79 'The Golden Key' (Bishop B062, April 2, 2026) is the canonical prose anchor. Battery Dispatch: 11 of 15 posts contain hidden keywords.
founder_voice_quotes: "Your attention has value. We're paying for it."
"It's a virtuous cycle, not an extraction machine."
"Nobody's gain is anybody's loss."

definition: Glowing Golden Keys is the community-density amplification layer built on top of the base Code Breakers / Golden Key system. As more members find the same key, the key changes visual state: Dim (0-9 finders, base value), Glowing (10-49 finders, +10%), Bright (50-99 finders, +25%), Radiant (100-499 finders, +50%), Legendary (500+ finders, +100%). The visual effect is literal: popular keys glow brighter in the interface, creating a community signal of which documents matter most. Beyond bonus MARKS, Glowing keys unlock Midas Touch tokens — stored abilities to convert 1 Credit into 1 Joule (Credits = spending currency; Joules = ownership containers). Midas token scale: Glowing (50+ finders) = 1 token, Bright (100+ finders) = 2 tokens, Radiant (500+ finders) = 3 tokens, Legendary (1,000+ finders) = 5 tokens. The 24-hour post-revelation of the clue in reply threads drives re-engagement — people return to see the answer, algorithmically re-surfacing the original content. The key system is deliberately modest in rewards (5-25 MARKS at base, scaling to milestone bonuses) — the point is not the reward but the engagement mechanic that creates value for participants instead of extracting it.
composing_primitives: Midas Touch token system (Credit-to-Joule conversion right)
Joule as 'ownership container' (same material as Credits but holds things)
Battery Dispatch integration (11 of 15 posts contain keys)
Non-zero-sum economics (finding by one does not prevent finding by others)

founder_voice_quotes: "The Glow comes from the community. The Touch is yours to use."
"Engagement mechanics can create value for participants without extracting it."

definition: Deck Cards are the visual, interactive navigation layer of the Liana Banyan platform — the primary way members navigate portals, discover content, and unlock access. Each Deck Card has: card_code (unique), name, front_title, back_title, back_instructions, description, front_icon, card_type (access/quest/achievement/tool), rarity (common/uncommon/rare/epic/legendary), deep_link_url, and credit_cost. The system includes: (1) member_deck table tracking which cards a member has unlocked and their slot_position; (2) deck_recipes for combining cards; (3) golden_keys table for member key inventory. Level system added in B089/B090 expansion. Key thresholds: level column (integer, default 1) gates progressive access. Portal-specific deck cards act as keys to portal entry (Portal Archway system — K422). The Triple Double Ladder Deck Card shows rung progression: Rung 0 $24K/yr → Rung 1 $48K/yr → Rung 2 $96K/yr → Rung 3 $192K/yr (each rung = 2× prior). The Medallion is a special deck card variant (K144 addendum). Mirror Mirror multilingual deck card system supports 110 languages (K373). Deep-link routing wires each card to its destination via card_code anchor. Lock threshold: a card's lock/unlock status is determined by the member_deck join — if no row exists for (member_id, card_id), the card is locked.
composing_primitives: Deck Card schema (card_code, rarity, credit_cost, deep_link_url)
Medallion as special Deck Card variant (K144)
Mirror Mirror multilingual system (110 languages, K373)
Auto Shop Deck Card (currency vocabulary trainer — K421)

definition: Three-gear currency system. CREDITS: Exchange rate $1 = 1 Credit. One-Way Valve — Credits NEVER cash out to fiat. Used for marketplace transactions. Per-person cap $5,000. Closed-loop, non-redeemable-for-fiat utility token; all regulatory MTL risk neutralized in TX and cooperative-friendly states if no fiat off-ramp exists. MARKS: Earned through effort and contribution — NOT purchased. Effort-differential currency. One-way ratchet: value only goes up, never down. All Marks are backed. Marks carry governance weight in the Round Table. Used to reward platform contribution beyond direct commerce. Legal character: internal unit of account tied to member patronage history — analogous to capital credit certificates. Voucher denominated in Marks = patronage allocation notice (Subchapter T framing). JOULES: Surplus / 'forever stamp' currency. Represents stored value from excess production. IMMUTABLE face value once minted — face_value persists through all transfers, only removed from circulation at redemption. Minted from Marks-surplus per backing rule (Pod-N Gold Tablet). Transferable. Redeemable for civilization-class work incentive. Legal character: prepaid forward contract for services / utility token. Joules = ownership containers ('same cloth as Credits, but a bag can hold things'). BACKED MARKS: Asset-backed cooperative equity units. Backing asset determines legal character — commodity forward (if backed by physical goods), investment contract (if backing appreciates with passive profit expected), cooperative equity certificates (if backed by fixed assets redeemable in kind). Structural recommendation: back with cooperative fixed assets or member-contributed productive capacity. HexIsle uses the same three currencies (Credits/Marks/Joules) — effort in the game generates value recognized outside it. Why three currencies don't create arbitrage: each serves a distinct function; Marks backing ratchet prevents devaluation; Credits one-way valve prevents extraction.
composing_primitives: Marks (effort-differential, all-backed, one-way ratchet, governance weight)
Joules (forever-stamp, immutable face value, minted from Marks-surplus)
WIR Bank precedent (Switzerland 1934, 92+ years cooperative multi-currency)
Subchapter T (Marks-denominated Vouchers as patronage allocation, not loans)
Pod-N Gold Tablet (Mark backing rule canonical pointer)
Midas Touch (Glowing key → Credit-to-Joule conversion token)

definition: HexIsle is the flagship demonstration of the Tereno Platform — a tabletop RPG that doubles as a Real-World Business Simulator. Core principle: HexIsle + Liana Banyan = Real-World Business Simulator with minimal cost outlay ($5 membership vs. $50K-$500K traditional). Geography: hexagonal grid where every hex has exactly 6 neighbors, all equidistant — geometry enforces fairness structurally. South-to-north archipelago journey across 7 islands (by Creative Director James Ausbin): Island 1 (Harvest) → 2 → 3 → 4 → 5 (Seek, caldera ring of 5 islets) → 6 (Magic, lost city rising from center of caldera) → 7. Terrain types: ocean, shore, grass, rock, cliff, snow, forest, volcanic, ruins, magic, stump, storm, ice, machine, stone, canal. Building archetypes include: hexagon (central iconic structure), tavern (Ghost World staging area), academy (education), forge (manufacturing), storefront (rentable retail), inn (accommodation + Airbnb bridge), rental_plot (empty rentable hex). FocusShell interface: full-screen immersive layout stripped of platform navigation chrome. Ghost World mechanic: practice/de-risk mode for real-world learning before commitment. Ghost Items: purchasable virtual items connecting to real products (weapons/tools/consumables/armor/accessories); 89,000+ model variants per item type. Skills: CANNOT be purchased — earned only through gameplay and real-world assignments. Economic model: same three currencies as main platform (Credits/Marks/Joules) — in-game effort generates value recognized outside the game. HexIsle has a Spanish-language sibling: Hexislo.com (parallel surface, same mechanics). Kickstarter integration for campaign funding. Member-operated island storefronts. The hex grid geometry is not decorative — equal adjacency enforces no inherently advantaged position by shape.
composing_primitives: Tereno Platform (F.M.C. system for game creation with real-world contract connections)
Hexagonal geometry (equal adjacency, fairness by structure)
Ghost Items vs. Skills distinction (purchasable vs. earned)

Knight sessions: K335 (character de-personification), K348 (Northern Province — past Snow Gate), K349 (Snow Gate Quest Chain), Knight Session 376 (HexIsle Phase 2 Treasure Map).

definition: The Voucher system (B62, Open Water architecture) is a seven-tier cooperative member advance framework enabling members to extend platform resource advances to fellow members to fund access to productive Vessel tiers, in exchange for SAA governance weight accruing to the backer. Seven tiers (Vessel / Range / Primary Legal Character): Tier 0 Dinghy $0-$50 de minimis peer advance; Tier 1 Rowboat $50-$500 time-price differential layaway/patronage advance; Tier 2 Canoe $500-$5,000 commercial loan (with affidavit)/consumer loan (without); Tier 3 Skiff $5,000-$15,000 commercial loan; Tier 4 Sailboat $15,000-$50,000 regulated commercial loan; Tier 5 Ship $50,000-$500,000 private placement/regulated commercial loan; Tier 6 Yacht $500,000+ securities instrument (Reg D 506(c) required). Four-layer structural defense: (1) Commercial Purpose Affidavit (removes TILA consumer framing); (2) Time-Price Differential structuring (4-installment max for Tiers 0-2); (3) Patronage Allocation Framing (Subchapter T WNA for Tiers 2-5); (4) Securities Safe Harbor (Reg D for Tiers 5-6). SAA (governance weight) is the return — NOT monetary profit; this defeats Howey prong 3. Strongest single action: Subchapter T election (simultaneously defeats lending law, securities, creates tax advantage, aligns incentives). Compliance cost: $63K-$153K build + $10K-$25K/year ongoing. Three phases: Phase 1 (Tiers 0-2, $8K-$23K, 1-3 months), Phase 2 (Tiers 3-4, $20K-$50K, 3-9 months), Phase 3 (Tiers 5-6, $35K-$80K, 6-18 months). California 5-loan de minimis cap is a hard operational trigger requiring database enforcement.
composing_primitives: Commercial Purpose Affidavit (TILA defeat at any dollar amount)
Reg D Rule 504 (Tier 5 securities safe harbor)
Reg D Rule 506(c) (Tier 6, verified accredited investors only)
California CFL 5-loan de minimis cap (database trigger)
Texas HB 700 OCCC registration (sales-based financing threshold $50K)
ratification_chronology: Pawn B62 Voucher Legal Characterization delivered April 11, 2026 (B099 ingest). 43 Parts + 12 Appendices. Model: Gemini 3.1 Pro Thinking. founder_voice_quotes: "The Subchapter T election is the single highest-ROI structural action."

definition: Pedestal Stake is an equity-adjacent instrument representing a claim on a specific slice of the patent-portfolio cash flow — specifically the 10% Individual Pedestals slice of the IP Load Balancing waterfall. Issuing entity: Upekrithen LLC (Wyoming, 100% owned by Founder), NOT Liana Banyan Corporation. This separation is structurally critical: Pedestal Stakes are securities; LB cooperative membership ($5/year) is not. Two-track economy: Marks (cooperative governance) and Pedestal Stakes (investment) are completely separate instruments. Holding a Pedestal Stake conveys NO LB cooperative voting rights; cooperative membership conveys NO Pedestal Stake. Four SEC exemption paths evaluated: Reg D 506(b) (accredited + ≤35 friends-and-family, no advertising), Reg D 506(c) (accredited only, can advertise, third-party verification required), Reg CF (up to $5M/12mo, general public, registered funding portal required), Reg A+ (up to $75M, mini-IPO, highest legal cost ~$100K). Bishop recommendation: Reg CF for consumer flow + Reg D 506(b) for accredited insiders parallel track. Howey-clean at B098 per Path A / Open Water / SAA framework vetting. Consumer flow infrastructure (not yet live as of B111, target Q3 2026): signup → KYC → payment → stake-issuance → investor statement dashboard. K427 builds platform portal UI. Concurrent counsel task: Offering Memorandum/Form C/PPM + Subscription Agreement + KYC/AML path + ongoing compliance + investor-communication FAQ. Public communication guardrail: do NOT offer Pedestal Stakes in Eyewitness Program recruitment until Form filed. $900M-$1.3B valuation projection is internal-tracking only (not for public use before offering filed). Reg CF requires funding portal (Wefunder/Republic/StartEngine/NextSeed options). Annual Reg CF compliance: Form C-AR + audited financials if raise > $535K.
composing_primitives: IP Load Balancing waterfall (Patent Buckets 60% / Founder-Creator 20% / Global Sponsor Pool 10% / Individual Patent Pedestals 10%)
Upekrithen LLC (Wyoming, issuing entity, separate from LB Corp)

KYC/AML integration (Tier 5+ and Pedestal Stake offering)
Herjavec RPA (50% of Founder-Creator 20% slice = 10% of total, 20-year window)

definition: Puzzle and code design patterns in the Liana Banyan ecosystem serve engagement without extraction. Three-tier difficulty architecture: Fledgling (word appears naturally in text — hidden in plain sight, easiest), Flight (first letters of sentences spell the key — acrostic pattern, medium), Murder (section headings spell the key — structural pattern, hardest). Comprehension questions supplement pattern keys for documents where understanding matters more than scanning (letters, academic papers): Basic (3 feathers — e.g., 'What percentage goes to creators?'), Applied (5 feathers — e.g., 'Why does Cost+20% eliminate the race to the bottom?'), Synthesis (7 feathers — e.g., 'How does the Boaz Principle differ from charity?'). Historical puzzle design: Battery Dispatch 15-post sequence with 11 embedded Golden Keys. 24-hour hint revelation drives return engagement. Grand Slam achievement (all 45 keys across five circles = Medallion + 5,000 MARKS) represents the full-corpus engagement target. Non-zero-sum principle: keys don't disappear after first find — infinite-harvest design. Crow Feathers as intermediate unit (1-7 per key based on difficulty) converting to MARKS at thresholds. The Five Circles (document types hosting keys): Press Articles, Influencer Letters, Academic Papers, Critique Responses, Cue Cards. Design philosophy: proven engagement (key submission proves careful reading) vs. vanity metrics (page views prove nothing). Community scouts: finders share discoveries, creating conversations, creating community, creating value — the social flywheel of the Code Breakers system.
composing_primitives: Crow Feathers (intermediate conversion unit, 1-7 per key)
Grand Slam achievement (45 keys = Medallion + 5,000 MARKS)
Community scouts (finders share → conversations → community → value)
ratification_chronology: Canonical ratification: ARTICLE_WHY_CODE_BREAKERS_MATTER_HUMANIZED.md (two engagement modes: Pattern Keys and Comprehension Questions). Pudding #79 The Golden Key (Bishop B062). Implementation: platform/src/components/treasure-map/TreasureMapKnowledgeQuiz.tsx and treasureMapEngine.ts. Acrostic pattern example: 'HUNT' spelled by first letters of sentences in 'Keys Get Brighter' section of Code Breakers article.
founder_voice_quotes: "A thousand page views tells us nothing. A hundred key submissions tells us people are reading carefully enough to notice patterns."
"Anyone can find a hidden word with enough patience. But answering 'Why does wealth re-accumulation threaten cooperative models?' requires actual engagement with the ideas."
